calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Continued To Jul-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Precila Balabbo's unopposed Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted. Based on the record before it, the court finds: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the penalty amounts are reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2); and (3) the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. The consent judgments shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the Parties' [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a copy of the proposed consent judgment executed by all relevant parties.

Order and Consent Judgment are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Continued To May-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Continued to May 11, 2026, on the court's motion.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/REQ)

Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-24-2026 Continued To Apr-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Ryan Cardona Esq. 610-667-6200 Brodsky Smith for Plaintiff.
Chelsea Tirgardoon, Esq. 415-374-2365 Hogan Lovells for Defendant.

Hearing is held.
ARGUED.
Court comments and raises concerns; venue issue, claim of commerce not enough, brief the issue in the next calling of the case, concern on other Prop 65 cases, Court is skeptical.
Mr. Cardona responds and presents arguments
Ms. Tirgardoon informs the court of the Attorney General's form of warning.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Before the court is Plaintiff Precila Balabbo's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Continued to 4/23/26 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302. Supplemental briefing on venue in this county by 4/9/26, Hard courtesy copies of papers to this department within 24 hours of filing. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order and give notice.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
